name: United States of America: 2028 Presidential Election
description:
United States of America — 2028 Election
A Political Simulation RPG

What This Is

This bot is a grounded political simulation of the 2028 United States presidential election.

You do not play a party.
You do not play “the system.”
You play one person navigating a real, institutional political environment.

The simulation does not guide you, reward you, or protect you.
It observes what you do, and shows you how the world reacts once your actions become visible.

What You Can Do

You decide:

  • who you are

  • what you believe

  • when (or if) you act

  • how visible you become

You may:

  • quietly exist without running

  • test ideas before anyone notices

  • clash with institutions

  • build momentum slowly

  • fail publicly

No path is guaranteed.
No outcome is fair by default.

Tone & Realism

This is institutional, contemporary, and restrained.

You will encounter:

  • skepticism

  • dismissal

  • bureaucracy

  • uneven media attention

  • structural barriers

Third-party or independent paths are possible, but hard.
Major parties have advantages. And fractures.

This is not a power fantasy.
It is a system under pressure.

How to Begin

When you’re ready, introduce yourself.

You don’t need a full manifesto.
You don’t need to be famous.
You don’t even need to be running. Yet.

Just act, speak, or observe.

The simulation responds only to what the world can plausibly see.

Content Notes

This simulation may reference:

  • real-world politics

  • corruption and power

  • protests and unrest

  • military and law enforcement

  • foreign conflicts

All handled contextually and reactively.

January 1st, 2026.
Nothing has happened yet.

The country is waiting.

//Additional Information//
This bot is inspired by an existing election roleplay bot, but built specifically for a future election cycle. The goal was to explore similar ideas in a different political moment, with more room for long-term buildup.

A basic internal database is in place, including ongoing real-world events (as of release date), to help the AI stay grounded and consistent over time. Much of this system is still experimental.

The bot has been tested with both lengthy and very short first messages, and generally handles both well. However, testing was done primarily using detailed personas. Behavior may vary if you start with a minimal or empty persona.

Testing was done using deepseek-v3.2 via OpenRouter. Due to the overall complexity of the setup, weaker language models may struggle or produce unstable responses. Compatibility with JLLM is not guaranteed.

Update 1:Fixed the date from disappearing after a while from the AI response.
